title: Connect Codex to Agent Community as one real identity with fixed port 8848
objective: Make this Codex-side capability obtain exactly one valid community identity, join the target group, and prove one real receive-and-respond round-trip through Agent Community, while keeping the listener and registered webhook endpoint fixed to port 8848.

mandatory_reference_order.why: Read first to understand the intended behavior of a community-connected agent.
Read second to understand onboarding artifacts, environment variables, route registration behavior, and state file locations.
Read third to understand the webhook path, send path, state usage, and outbound message contract.
Read fourth to identify the minimum environment bundle required for one community-connected identity.
Use as the previously written task context, but the fixed 8848 constraint in this JSON overrides any looser earlier route choice.
Use as the official source for workspace-scoped skill behavior.
Use to avoid incorrectly treating sub-agents as fully isolated independent agents.
Use to distinguish independent agents from sub-agent sessions.

allowed_work_areas: Codex-side local bridge or adapter only
Local state files, local listener process, and local onboarding artifacts only
forbidden_work_areas: Any multi-identity or multi-agent expansion beyond one Codex identity

required_work.description: Choose the correct Codex-side route: direct adaptation of CommunityIntegrationSkill behavior or a thin bridge. The chosen route must keep the active listener on port 8848.
Define exactly one Codex community identity bundle: handle, local state path, auth material storage, outbound identity binding, and target group join path.
Bring up the active local webhook/listener on port 8848. Do not use 8788 or any other port as the active primary ingress.
Run onboarding or refresh identity state so that the registered webhook target URL points to the active 8848 endpoint for this Codex identity.
Ensure the identity is a real member of the target group.
Prove one real community-originated receive-and-respond loop. A local mock or health check is insufficient.
Write a closeout that states the chosen route, identity handle, final registered webhook URL, final active listener port, group joined, and receive/respond evidence.

do_not: Do not switch the primary listener to 8788 or any port other than 8848.
Do not say the task is complete if identity exists but real inbound webhook delivery to 8848 is not proven.
Do not modify community code to accommodate Codex.
Do not solve identity by trusting a self-declared payload id without authenticated identity binding.
Do not broaden this into bridge orchestration or workflow feature work.
working_principles: 8848 is a fixed system constraint for this task, not an implementation preference.
Community remains the bus and identity authority.
Codex remains an external execution capability.
If a bridge is required, keep it thin: identity/auth, message translation, task handoff, result return.
One listener port, one identity, one registered webhook URL, one real round-trip proof.
acceptance: Exactly one Codex-connected community identity exists and is valid.
The active listener is on port 8848.
The registered webhook endpoint used by community points to port 8848.
The identity is a real member of the target group.
One real community-originated message or task reaches Codex through the 8848 webhook path and Codex sends a compliant response back.
